title: 11 Best Herbal Teas For Hyperacidity
intro: Herbal teas for Hyperacidity are a type of herbal remedy that helps alleviate symptoms of excessive stomach acid production, a condition characterized by heartburn, bloating, and discomfort. These teas can treat hyperacidity by soothing the digestive tract, reducing inflammation, and promoting relaxation, which in turn helps regulate stomach acid levels. Examples of herbal teas that can help treat hyperacidity include peppermint tea, which calms the digestive system and reduces inflammation; ginger tea, which aids digestion and reduces nausea; fennel tea, which soothes the stomach and alleviates bloating; licorice root tea, which protects the mucous membranes and reduces inflammation; chamomile tea, which promotes relaxation and reduces stress; and dandelion tea, which supports liver function and promotes digestion. By incorporating these herbal teas into one's diet, individuals can find natural relief from the discomforts associated with hyperacidity.

preparations.herb_desc: Zingiber officinale teas, derived from the rhizomes of the ginger plant, have been traditionally used to treat hyperacidity due to their antacid and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents, including gingerols and shogaols, help to neutralize stomach acid and reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, thereby alleviating symptoms of hyperacidity. By reducing inflammation and neutralizing stomach acid, Zingiber officinale teas help to treat hyperacidity by providing relief from heartburn, bloating, and discomfort. The benefits of using Zingiber officinale teas to treat hyperacidity include promoting digestive health, reducing symptoms of acid reflux, and providing a natural and non-invasive approach to managing the condition.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 teaspoon of dried Zingiber officinale root and 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the Zingiber officinale root in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey (optional) to the tea for taste.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help manage hyperacidity.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Zingiber officinale teas can be used to treat hyperacidity ailment, but may cause side effects such as stomach upset, nausea, and diarrhea in some individuals due to its potent ginger content. To minimize potential side effects, it's recommended to start with a low dose, consume the tea in moderation, and avoid taking it on an empty stomach, especially for those with sensitive stomachs or pre-existing digestive issues.
preparation_usage: Consult a doctor for Zingiber officinale tea dosage and frequency.
Drink 1 cup of Zingiber officinale tea 2-3 times a day.
Use 1-2 teaspoons of Zingiber officinale powder per cup.
Start with small doses and adjust as per doctor's advice.
Monitor acid reflux symptoms and adjust tea usage accordingly.

preparations.herb_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra teas have been traditionally used to treat hyperacidity ailments due to their anti-inflammatory and carminative properties. The herbal preparation helps to neutralize stomach acid, reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, and alleviate symptoms such as heartburn and bloating. Glycyrrhiza glabra teas contain bioactive constituents like glycyrrhizin, a triterpenoid saponin that helps to soothe the mucous membranes in the stomach and reduce acid production, thereby providing relief from hyperacidity. Regular consumption of Glycyrrhiza glabra teas can lead to numerous benefits, including reduced symptoms of hyperacidity, improved digestion, and enhanced overall gut health.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Glycyrrhiza glabra root, a large pot, a fine-mesh sieve, and a cup.
Boil 2 cups of water in the large pot and remove from heat.
Add 1 tablespoon of dried Glycyrrhiza glabra root to the hot water and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the liquid through the fine-mesh sieve into the cup, pressing on the solids to extract all the liquid.
Drink the tea slowly, 1/2 cup at a time, and repeat as needed to alleviate hyperacidity symptoms.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Molecules (Basel, Switzerland)", Glycyrrhiza glabra teas for hyperacidity show no health risk for both ingestion and topical application due to low co-extraction of trace elements and nontoxic NADES extracts of the roots.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Glycyrrhiza glabra teas can be used to treat hyperacidity, but they may cause side effects such as high blood pressure, low potassium levels, and increased risk of heart problems due to the licorice root's aldosterone-like effects. Precautions should be taken when using these teas, such as limiting consumption to short periods of time and avoiding them altogether for those with pre-existing heart conditions, high blood pressure, or kidney disease, as well as pregnant or breastfeeding women.
preparation_usage: Drink 1/2 cup Glycyrrhiza glabra tea 2 times a day.
Start with 1 teaspoon dried root per cup of boiling water.
Gradually increase to 2 teaspoons if symptoms persist after 7 days.
Use for 2-3 weeks to manage hyperacidity, then take a 1-week break.
Consult a healthcare professional for personalized guidance and dosage.

preparations.herb_desc: Curcuma longa teas have been traditionally used to treat hyperacidity by virtue of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to neutralize stomach acid and soothe digestive issues. This herbal preparation aids in treating hyperacidity by promoting digestion, reducing inflammation, and enhancing gut health, thereby providing relief from symptoms such as heartburn and bloating. The bioactive constituents of Curcuma longa teas, including curcumin, demethoxycurcumin, and bisdemethoxycurcumin, possess potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to combat oxidative stress and inflammation in the digestive system. The benefits of using Curcuma longa teas to treat hyperacidity include reduced inflammation, improved digestion, and enhanced overall gut health, making it a popular natural remedy for this common ailment.

Wash the Curcuma longa root with water and chop it into small pieces.
Boil the water in the saucepan, then add the chopped Curcuma longa root.
Reduce the heat and let it simmer for 10-15 minutes, then strain the mixture.

preparations.preparation_study: According to the study, Curcuma longa teas for hyperacidity may be beneficial due to its high concentration of antioxidants and inhibitory action against most bacterial strains, which can help in healing gastrointestinal infections.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Curcuma longa teas can be beneficial in treating hyperacidity, but some possible side effects include gastrointestinal upset, such as diarrhea and stomach cramps, which may be due to the high doses of curcumin in the tea. To minimize these side effects, it's essential to use the tea in moderation, avoid mixing it with other medications, and drink plenty of water to stay hydrated, as excessive consumption can exacerbate the condition.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Curcuma longa tea after meals to reduce hyperacidity.
Take 1-2 teaspoons of Curcuma longa powder with 1 cup of water twice a day.
Start with low dose (1 teaspoon) for first 3 days and increase to 2 teaspoons.
Drink Curcuma longa tea for 2-3 months to see noticeable improvement in hyperacidity.
Monitor your progress and adjust dosage as needed under doctor's guidance.

Drink 1-2 cups of Cinnamomum verum tea 2-3 times a day

Drink the tea warm, 2-3 times a day to help with hyperacidity.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Elettaria cardamomum teas can be an effective herbal remedy for treating hyperacidity, but it may also cause side effects such as stomach upset, diarrhea, and allergic reactions in some individuals. To use Elettaria cardamomum teas safely, it is essential to take precautions like starting with small doses, avoiding consumption before meals, and being cautious when combining with other medications or supplements.

preparations.herb_desc: Cynara scolymus teas, also known as artichoke tea, have been used for centuries to treat hyperacidity ailments due to their soothing and protective properties. The herbal preparation helps to treat this ailment by reducing inflammation in the stomach and esophagus, while also promoting the production of digestive enzymes that help to break down food efficiently. The bioactive constituents, including flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic acids, found in Cynara scolymus teas, work together to neutralize excess stomach acid and protect the mucous membranes from damage. The benefits of this herbal preparation in treating hyperacidity ailments include reduced symptoms of heartburn, acid reflux, and stomach pain, as well as improved overall digestive health and well-being.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried artichoke leaves (Cynara scolymus) and 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the artichoke leaves in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture and discard the leaves.
Add honey to taste and mix well.
Drink 1 cup of the tea, 2-3 times a day, to help soothe hyperacidity.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Cynara scolymus teas can be used to treat hyperacidity, but possible side effects include digestive discomfort, nausea, and allergic reactions in some individuals, particularly those with sensitive stomachs or allergies. Precautions to take when using Cynara scolymus teas include starting with small doses, avoiding consumption before bedtime, and being mindful of potential interactions with other medications or health conditions, such as kidney or liver issues.

preparations.herb_desc: Centella asiatica teas have been traditionally used to treat hyperacidity ailments due to their soothing and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of this herbal preparation, such as triterpenoids and flavonoids, help to neutralize stomach acid and reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, thereby alleviating the symptoms of hyperacidity. The benefits of Centella asiatica teas in treating hyperacidity include reduced heartburn, bloating, and discomfort, as well as improved digestion and gut health. Regular consumption of Centella asiatica teas may also help to prevent the recurrence of hyperacidity and promote overall well-being.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried Centella asiatica leaves and 1 cup of water.
Heat the water in a pot and bring it to a boil.
Reduce the heat and add the dried Centella asiatica leaves. Let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth. Discard the leaves.
Drink 1/2 cup of the tea 2-3 times a day to help alleviate hyperacidity symptoms.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Journal of ethnobiology and ethnomedicine, Centella asiatica teas for hyperacidity, which was processed using various techniques from green tea, black tea, and white tea, exhibited variations in characteristics and were found to be suitable for consumption.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Centella asiatica teas can be used to treat hyperacidity, but its prolonged use may lead to side effects such as stomach upset, diarrhea, and nausea due to its high mucilaginous and alkaloid content. To minimize these side effects, it is essential to consume Centella asiatica teas in moderation, ideally 1-2 cups per day, and to avoid taking it on an empty stomach or with other medications that may interact with its active compounds.

preparations.herb_desc: Zingiber zerumbet teas have been traditionally used to treat hyperacidity due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents, such as gingerols and shogaols, present in these teas help to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of hyperacidity, including heartburn and acid reflux. By inhibiting the production of stomach acid and improving digestion, Zingiber zerumbet teas provide relief from hyperacidity, promoting a healthy gut and reducing discomfort. Regular consumption of these teas also offers additional benefits, including improved gut health, reduced nausea, and a boost to the immune system.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Zingiber zerumbet rhizomes and 2 cups of water.
Wash the rhizomes and chop them into small pieces.
Boil the 2 cups of water in a pot.
Add 1 tablespoon of chopped Zingiber zerumbet rhizomes to the boiling water and reduce heat.
Steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain and drink as tea, 2-3 times a day.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Zingiber zerumbet teas can potentially cause digestive discomfort, such as nausea and diarrhea, in some individuals, especially when consumed in large quantities or for prolonged periods. Precautions should be taken to avoid exacerbating the hyperacidity ailment, including drinking plenty of water to stay hydrated and avoiding spicy or acidic foods while consuming the herbal preparation.

preparations.herb_desc: Silybum marianum teas, also known as milk thistle tea, have been traditionally used to treat hyperacidity due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents, such as silymarin and flavonoids, help to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, alleviate symptoms of hyperacidity, and promote healing of the mucous membranes. By soothing the stomach lining and reducing acid production, Silybum marianum teas provide relief from heartburn, bloating, and discomfort associated with hyperacidity. Regular consumption of this herbal preparation can also help to normalize digestion, improve gut health, and reduce the risk of complications associated with hyperacidity.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried Silybum marianum flowers.
Boil 1 cup of water in a pot.
Add the 2 tablespoons of dried flowers to the boiling water.
Reduce heat and let it simmer for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup and drink while warm.
preparations.preparation_study: According to European journal of drug metabolism and pharmacokinetics, Silybum marianum teas for hyperacidity may be less effective in terms of reducing power property and total scavenger capacity compared to an extract of Helichrysum arenarium (L.)
